dashboard.live_overview_hint: Check readiness, run state, and the strongest signal before reading the chart.
dashboard.active_car_none_no_cars: No cars added yet - add one to enable recording.
dashboard.active_car_none_blocked: No active car selected - choose or create one to enable recording.
dashboard.logging.waiting: Connect sensors to begin a trustworthy run.
dashboard.logging.active_car_required: Recording is blocked until you select or create an active car.
dashboard.logging.unassigned: {count} live sensor(s) still need a location.
dashboard.logging.frame_loss: Frame loss is being detected on {count} sensor(s).
dashboard.logging.offline: {count} sensor(s) are offline, so coverage is incomplete.
dashboard.logging.running: Recording is active with {connected} online sensor(s) and {assigned} assigned location(s). Press Stop Recording to save the run and start analysis.
dashboard.logging.stopping: Stopping the run and saving captured samples...
dashboard.logging.processing: Run {runId} was saved. Analysis is in progress.
dashboard.logging.processing.title: Run {runId} is being analyzed.
dashboard.logging.processing.body: Results will appear in History as soon as processing finishes.
dashboard.logging.processing.detail: Open History now if you want to watch for the completed diagnosis.
dashboard.logging.saved: Run {runId} was saved and is ready in History.
dashboard.logging.saved.title: Run {runId} is ready in History.
dashboard.logging.saved.body: Open History to review the diagnosis, heatmap, and exports.
dashboard.logging.saved.detail: You can start another run from Live at any time.
dashboard.logging.blocked.no_cars.title: Add a car before recording.
dashboard.logging.blocked.no_cars.body: Runs need an active car so History and analysis stay tied to the right setup.
dashboard.logging.blocked.no_cars.detail: Open Cars to create the first profile and unlock Start Recording.
dashboard.logging.blocked.no_active.titl: Choose the active car before recording.
dashboard.logging.blocked.no_active.body: This setup already has cars, but none is active for the next run.
dashboard.logging.blocked.no_active.deta: Open Cars, activate the right profile, then return to Live to start recording.
dashboard.logging.blocked.setup.title: Finish setup before recording.
dashboard.logging.blocked.setup.detail: Current blocker: {step}. Live unlocks Start Recording as soon as this step passes.
dashboard.capture_readiness.title: Capture readiness checklist
dashboard.capture_readiness.summary.read: Everything needed for a steady-state recording is ready.
dashboard.capture_readiness.sensors_read: {count} live sensor(s) still need a location.
Wait {seconds}s after recent frame loss or parse errors.
Only {count} live sensor(s) are active, so location ranking will be weaker.
{count} live sensor(s) are assigned and streaming cleanly.
dashboard.capture_readiness.reference_re: Choose an active car before recording.
Complete the active car's tire and driveline settings first.
No live speed source is available yet.
Choose GPS, OBD-II, or a manual speed before starting steady-state capture.
Waiting for live speed updates instead of fallback speed.
Waiting for a fresh live speed update.
Waiting for a valid live speed reading.
Waiting for a fresh OBD-II RPM sample.
Live speed and rotational reference data are ready.
dashboard.capture_readiness.speed_stable: Waiting for a live speed sample.
Hold a steady cruise above {minimumSpeed} km/h.
Keep the speed steady for {seconds}s more.
Speed is still moving around; settle into cruise.
Speed has been stable long enough to capture.
dashboard.capture_readiness.capture_read: Finish the remaining checklist items first.
Recording can start, but review the cautions above.
dashboard.matrix_note: Levels use peak-over-floor dB bands (L1-L5), grouped by source type.
dashboard.rotational.title: Rotational Speeds
history.empty.title: Capture the first run from Live.
history.empty.body: History fills automatically after you stop and save a recording.
history.empty.detail: Open Live to start a run and come back here for diagnosis and exports.
history.delete_confirm: Delete run "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
history.delete_all_confirm: Delete all {count} run(s)? This cannot be undone.
history.preview_unavailable: Preview is unavailable for this run.
history.raw_capture_missing_detail: This run summary is still available, but the raw waveform capture was pruned by retention maintenance and cannot be replayed.
history.raw_capture_degraded_enqueue_tim: Raw capture finalization could not be queued before stop completed. Queue depth at failure: {queueDepth}. Error: {errorSummary}.
history.raw_capture_degraded_timeout_det: Raw capture finalization did not finish before the bounded stop timeout. Queue depth at failure: {queueDepth}. Error: {errorSummary}.
history.raw_capture_degraded_failed_deta: Raw capture finalization failed before the run closed. Queue depth at failure: {queueDepth}. Error: {errorSummary}.
history.run_actions_body: After reviewing the diagnosis, export the raw data or delete the run if it is no longer needed.
history.findings_pending: Detailed diagnosis will appear automatically once analysis is ready.
history.findings_ready: Diagnosis and strongest evidence are shown below.
history.inconclusive_title: Insufficient evidence to name the source
history.inconclusive_body: This run showed vibration activity, but the evidence stayed too mixed to support an inspection-first diagnosis.
history.inconclusive_next_step: Repeat the run with live speed and full sensor coverage before treating one component as the cause.
report.unable_load_insights: Unable to load insights for selected run.
report.no_findings_for_run: No findings generated for this run.
settings.car.hint: Add cars from the library or enter specs manually. Activate a car to use it for analysis.
settings.car.incomplete_detail: Open Analysis to finish the missing tire and drivetrain specs before using this car.
settings.car.created_body: {name} was added and selected for this setup.
settings.car.created_detail: Review the highlighted row below or open Analysis to confirm the setup before the next run.
settings.car.empty.title: Add the first car profile.
settings.car.empty.body: Cars define the setup used for recording, saved runs, and analysis settings.
settings.car.empty.detail: Start with the add-car wizard so the next recording has the right context.
settings.car.guidance.no_cars: Add a car below to unlock analysis settings.
settings.car.guidance.no_active_title: Activate one car for this setup.
settings.car.guidance.no_active: Activate a car from the list below or add a new one to unlock analysis settings.
settings.car.guidance.no_active_detail: Use Activate on a ready row, or Finish setup on an incomplete row, to unlock the rest of Settings.
settings.car.delete_confirm: Delete car "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
settings.car.activate_incomplete: Finish the car setup in Analysis before activating it.
settings.car.wizard_intro: Use the library when it fits, or branch into manual specs without losing your place.
settings.car.wizard_task_intro: This flow pauses the rest of Settings so you can build the next analysis profile step by step without losing your place.
settings.car.wizard_summary_intro: Your choices stay visible here while the profile comes together.
settings.car.library_branch_note: Choose the tire and gearbox that match this car. Finish stays pinned below.
settings.car.manual_model_note: Skip library variants and finish with your own wheel and gearbox values.
settings.car.branch_divider: Or switch to the manual branch
settings.car.manual_specs_note: Use this branch when the library stops short or you already know the wheel and gearbox measurements.
settings.car.finish_choose_path: Choose a library gearbox or edit the manual specs to finish.
settings.car.finish_library_ready: Library path selected. Add Car will use the highlighted tire and gearbox.
settings.car.finish_library_approximate: Library path selected, but the drivetrain ratios are approximate for this variant. Add Car will keep them marked as approximate so you can confirm them later.
settings.car.finish_manual_ready: Manual path selected. Add Car will use the values entered below.
settings.car.finish_manual_missing: Enter positive tire and gearbox values to finish.
settings.car.approximate_detail: This car still uses approximate drivetrain ratios from a family-default library match. Review or override them in Analysis before trusting driveshaft or engine order results.
settings.car.confidence.review_detail: Review or override in Analysis before trusting driveshaft or engine order results.
settings.analysis.guidance_summary: Keep the defaults unless the data is unusually noisy or the vehicle specs are approximate.
settings.analysis.guidance_intro: Most users should keep the defaults. Use wider bands or higher uncertainty only when your data is unusually noisy or your vehicle specs are approximate.
settings.analysis.guidance_guardrail: Values outside the guided range will ask for confirmation before they are saved.
settings.analysis.no_car_selected: Select or create a car in Settings → Car to unlock analysis settings.
settings.analysis.reset_confirm: Replace the current analysis tuning values with the safe defaults for wheel, driveline, and uncertainty matching?
settings.sensors.title: Sensors
settings.sensors.hint: Manage sensor names and locations. Default name is the MAC address.
settings.sensors.name: Name
settings.speed.title: Speed Source
settings.speed.summary_caption: This source is currently driving the app. Changes below take effect after save.
settings.speed.gps_caption: Use live GPS speed when it is healthy and available.
settings.speed.gps_intro: Choose how long stale live-source data can remain usable before the manual fallback takes over.
settings.speed.obd_caption: Use a paired Bluetooth OBD adapter on the Pi for live vehicle speed and RPM.
settings.speed.obd_intro: Pair a Bluetooth OBD adapter with the Pi, then save OBD-II as the selected live source.
settings.speed.obd_scan_idle: Scan to discover nearby Bluetooth OBD adapters.
settings.speed.obd_scan_empty: No nearby Bluetooth OBD adapters found.
settings.speed.obd_missing_device_error: Pair a Bluetooth OBD adapter before saving OBD-II as the speed source.
settings.speed.manual_caption: Use a fixed speed when you need a deliberate override.
settings.speed.manual_intro: Set a fixed speed for manual mode and as the live-source fallback when GPS or OBD-II data goes stale.
settings.speed.manual_invalid: Enter a manual speed between 0.1 and 500 km/h.
settings.speed.save_failed_detail: {source} remains active right now. Your draft stays here so you can fix and retry.
settings.speed.validation_active_detail: {source} remains active right now. No changes were saved.
settings.speed.status_caption: Connection, freshness, effective speed, fallback diagnostics, and Bluetooth OBD detail when configured.
settings.speed.stale_timeout_invalid: Enter a stale timeout between 3s and 120s.
settings.preference.save_failed_active: {label} could not be saved. {value} remains active.
settings.analysis.group.order_band_width: These values control how far the app searches around each expected order. Wider bands tolerate more speed drift but can blend nearby faults together.
settings.analysis.group.uncertainty_mode: Use these only when vehicle data is approximate, modified, or worn. Higher uncertainty makes matching more tolerant, but it can lower specificity and confidence.
settings.analysis.invalid_value: {field} must stay between {min}{unit} and {max}{unit}. Current value: {value}{unit}.
settings.analysis.save_failed_detail: The previous saved analysis settings remain active. Your edits stay here so you can fix and retry.
settings.analysis.risky_confirm_intro: These values are outside the guided range for everyday use:
settings.analysis.risky_confirm_outro: Save anyway? Wider bands or higher uncertainty can make matching more tolerant, but they can also blur the diagnosis.
settings.uncertainty_defaults: Defaults use tire wear from 10/32 in to 2/32 in plus safety margin.
settings.validation_error: One or more analysis settings are out of range. Please check your values.
status.view_load_failed: This view could not be loaded right now.
ws.banner.stale: Data may be stale — no updates received recently
spectrum.controls_hint: Select a trace to isolate it. Show reference bands for order context.
spectrum.stale: Data is stale. Waiting for fresh frames.
spectrum.empty: Connected, but no spectrum frames yet.
spectrum.chart_load_error: Failed to load the spectrum chart: {message}
spectrum.frame_prepare_error: Failed to prepare the spectrum frame: {message}
spectrum.inspector_idle: Hover the chart or select a trace to inspect the peak.
vibration.db_scale_note: dB is measured above background noise floor.
chart.strength.title: Strength over time
settings.internet.title: Internet
settings.internet.hint: USB internet is optional. When a compatible phone or USB network device is detected, the Pi can keep its hotspot active while using USB as the upstream connection.
settings.internet.summary.usable: A USB-backed uplink is ready to use as upstream internet.
settings.internet.summary.detected: A USB-backed network interface is present, but it is not ready as an upstream yet.
settings.internet.summary.not_detected: No USB-backed network interface is currently detected.
settings.internet.load_failed: USB internet status could not be loaded right now.
settings.update.title: System Update
settings.update.hint: Use either temporary Wi-Fi credentials or an already-connected USB internet uplink to update from GitHub. The hotspot only pauses for the Wi-Fi path.
settings.update.preflight_note_wifi: Starting a Wi-Fi update temporarily pauses hotspot access while the Pi joins Wi-Fi, downloads the next release, and restores the local web UI when the job is done.
settings.update.preflight_note_usb: Starting a USB-internet update keeps the hotspot up and reuses the existing USB uplink while the Pi checks GitHub, downloads the next release, and installs it.
settings.update.controls_intro: Use Wi-Fi credentials as before, or choose the existing USB internet uplink when the Pi detects one.
settings.update.details_caption_wifi: Hotspot access pauses while the Pi joins Wi-Fi.
settings.update.details_caption_usb: The hotspot stays up while the USB uplink is reused.
settings.update.readiness.title: Start readiness
settings.update.readiness.summary_ready: All visible prerequisites are ready to start the update.
settings.update.readiness.summary_blocke: Complete the blocked item before starting the update.
settings.update.readiness.summary_runnin: The update is already running with the current prerequisites locked in.
settings.update.recovery.title: Recovery guidance
settings.update.recovery.summary_retry: The last update stopped before completion. Review the captured details, then retry when the connection path is ready.
settings.update.recovery.summary_blocked: The last update stopped before completion. Clear the blocked item before retrying.
settings.update.recovery.item.next_step_: Restore a valid transport path and background health before retrying the update.
settings.update.recovery.wifi.title: Reconnect the Wi-Fi path
settings.update.recovery.wifi.detail: Wait for hotspot access to return, then confirm the Wi-Fi network name and password before retrying.
settings.update.recovery.usb.title: Stabilize the USB uplink
settings.update.recovery.usb.detail: Reconnect or re-enable the USB internet source, then retry once the Pi reports the uplink as usable.
settings.update.recovery.network.title: Check upstream connectivity
settings.update.recovery.network.detail: Confirm the Pi can still reach GitHub through the selected connection path, then retry the update.
settings.update.recovery.install.title: Review install diagnostics
settings.update.recovery.install.detail: Inspect the issues and updater log below, then retry only after the runtime and storage problems are resolved.
settings.update.recovery.generic.title: Review the captured failure
settings.update.recovery.generic.detail: Use the issue details, latest attempt, and updater log below to understand the failure before retrying.
settings.update.readiness.item.source_wi: Temporary Wi-Fi is selected for this maintenance run.
settings.update.readiness.item.source_us: Existing USB internet is selected for this maintenance run.
settings.update.readiness.item.connectio: A Wi-Fi network name is entered and ready to use.
Enter a Wi-Fi SSID to enable Start Update.
Choose Temporary Wi-Fi or wait for a usable USB internet uplink.
settings.update.readiness.item.health_bl: Resolve the degraded background service issues below before starting an update.
settings.update.current_status_summary.r: Ready to start once the Pi has either temporary Wi-Fi credentials or a usable USB internet uplink.
settings.update.current_status_summary.a: Review the operational health notes below before starting an update.
settings.update.current_status_summary.r: An update is already in progress. Keep this page open until the hotspot returns.
settings.update.current_status_summary.s: The last update completed successfully. Review the installed version and timing before running another.
settings.update.current_status_summary.f: The last update did not finish cleanly. Review the latest status details and updater log before trying again.
settings.update.current_status_empty: No previous update details are stored yet. Once the first update runs, this panel will keep the latest status, version, and timing details.
settings.update.journey_intro: The updater works through these stages in order, so the current step and what comes next stay visible on one screen.
settings.update.journey.detail.validatin: Checks the request and current updater state before the Pi leaves hotspot mode.
settings.update.journey.detail.stopping_: Pauses hotspot access so the Pi can switch over to the temporary Wi-Fi network.
settings.update.journey.detail.connectin: Brings up the provided Wi-Fi link and confirms that the Pi can reach the network it needs.
Confirms that the detected USB uplink is ready before the Pi checks GitHub for the next release.
settings.update.journey.detail.checking: Contacts GitHub and verifies which release should be installed next.
settings.update.journey.detail.downloadi: Fetches the release package and related assets needed for the install.
settings.update.journey.detail.installin: Installs the new package, verifies the runtime, and records any issues that need attention.
settings.update.journey.detail.restoring: Brings the local hotspot and web UI back so this page can reconnect automatically.
settings.update.journey.detail.done: Records the result and last-success time so the next maintenance check starts with context.
settings.update.issues_intro: Recent validation or install problems that could affect the next run.
settings.update.issues_empty_body: If the next run raises a validation, Wi-Fi, or install problem, it will appear here with the stage that needs attention.
settings.update.log_intro: Recent updater output from the most recent run.
settings.update.log_intro_running: Live updater output from the active run.
settings.update.log_running_body: The update is active and new log lines will appear here as the updater emits them.
settings.update.log_running_note: Live stream: new updater lines appear here automatically while the update is running.
settings.update.log_failed_body: Use the failed step and captured issue details above to recover, then retry once the connection path is ready.
settings.update.log_empty_body: Once an update starts, this card keeps the latest updater output so you can confirm how far the maintenance run progressed.
settings.update.transport.wifi_summary: Pause the hotspot, join a Wi-Fi network, install, then restore the hotspot.
settings.update.transport.usb_summary: Reuse the currently detected USB uplink without taking the hotspot down.
settings.update.transport.usb_summary_in: Reuse the currently detected USB uplink on {interface}.
settings.update.transport.usb_summary_un: USB internet is not ready yet.
settings.update.attempt_intro: The most recent update result stays here so recovery details survive after the run stops.
settings.update.health.label: Operational health
settings.update.health_card_summary.ok: Core services look stable enough for a maintenance action.
settings.update.health_card_summary.warn: There are active warnings. Review them before taking the hotspot offline.
settings.update.health_card_summary.degr: The system is degraded. Resolve the issues below before attempting an update.
settings.update.already_running: An update is already in progress.
settings.update.usb_unavailable: USB internet is no longer ready. Refresh the status or switch back to Wi-Fi.
settings.update.reconnect_note: Note: The page may disconnect while the hotspot is down for the Wi-Fi path. It will reconnect automatically.
settings.update.guidance.prerequisites_i: A calm update page should answer the basics before the Pi leaves hotspot mode.
settings.update.guidance.prerequisites_1: Use a Wi-Fi network with internet access so the Pi can reach GitHub.
settings.update.guidance.prerequisites_2: Make sure you are ready to reconnect after hotspot access pauses for the update.
settings.update.guidance.prerequisites_3: Run updates when no critical recording or support session depends on the hotspot.
settings.update.guidance.outcome_intro: Starting an update triggers a short maintenance sequence on this Pi.
settings.update.guidance.outcome_1: The hotspot pauses while the Pi joins the Wi-Fi network you provide.
settings.update.guidance.outcome_2: The updater checks GitHub, downloads the release, and installs the new package.
settings.update.guidance.outcome_3: The hotspot and web UI return automatically when the update flow finishes.
settings.update.guidance.recovery_intro: Most update interruptions are recoverable from this page.
settings.update.guidance.recovery_1: Use the health and issues panels to see whether the blocker came from Wi-Fi, runtime validation, or package install steps.
settings.update.guidance.recovery_2: If the page disconnects, wait for the hotspot to come back before retrying.
settings.update.guidance.recovery_3: A successful run records the installed version and last completion time here for the next check.
settings.esp_flash.title: ESP Flash
settings.esp_flash.hint: Build and flash firmware from local source on this Pi.
settings.esp_flash.preflight_note: Starting a flash builds the latest firmware on this Pi, erases the selected board, and writes the new image over USB. Keep the board powered until the staged progress reaches Done.
settings.esp_flash.details_caption: Build, erase, and write the current firmware over USB.
settings.esp_flash.start_readiness.title: Start readiness
settings.esp_flash.start_readiness.summa: A flash target is ready and the job can start.
Connect the board and clear the blocked item before flashing.
The flash job is already running with the current target locked in.
Connect the ESP board over USB and refresh the port list.
Pick Auto-detect or a detected serial port after the board appears.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.title: Recovery guidance
settings.esp_flash.recovery.summary_retr: The last flash stopped before completion. Review the captured details, then retry when the board is ready.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.summary_bloc: The last flash stopped before completion. Reconnect the board and clear the blocked item before retrying.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.item.next_st: Reconnect the board over USB and refresh the port list before retrying the flash.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.fallback_err: The flash stopped before it could report a detailed error.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.validating.d: Confirm the selected board or auto-detected port is correct, then retry the flash.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.preparing.ti: Fix the build or bundle problem
settings.esp_flash.recovery.preparing.de: Review the live output and resolve the firmware bundle or toolchain problem before retrying.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.erasing.deta: Keep the ESP board powered, reconnect USB if needed, and retry only when the connection stays stable.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.flashing.det: Reconnect the board, confirm the selected port, and retry once the cable and power are stable.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.cancelled.de: Review the selected target and any live output, then start a new flash only when the board is ready.
settings.esp_flash.recovery.generic.deta: Use the failed step, recent attempt, and log output below to understand the problem before retrying.
settings.esp_flash.history_intro: Recent flashes stay here so the next operator can see what happened last.
settings.esp_flash.journey.detail.valida: Checks that a firmware bundle is available and that the selected board or auto-detected port is usable.
settings.esp_flash.journey.detail.prepar: Loads the cached firmware bundle on the Pi and prepares the flash commands for this board.
settings.esp_flash.journey.detail.erasin: Clears the target flash so the new firmware can be written cleanly.
settings.esp_flash.journey.detail.flashi: Writes the current firmware image over USB and streams the tool output into the live log panel.
settings.esp_flash.journey.detail.done: Stores the outcome and last-success time so the next operator can confirm what happened last.
settings.esp_flash.journey_terminal.fail: The last flash stopped before the remaining stages could finish. Review the error, log output, and latest attempt before trying again.
settings.esp_flash.journey_terminal.canc: The last flash was cancelled before the remaining stages could finish. Confirm the selected target and restart only when the board is ready.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.rea: The Pi can see at least one serial device. Confirm the target and start when the board is ready.
No serial device is detected yet. Connect the ESP32 by USB and refresh before starting.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.run: Flashing is in progress. Keep the board powered and connected until the status returns.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.suc: The most recent flash completed successfully. You can review the last result and flash again if needed.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.fai: The last flash ended with an error. Review the last attempt and logs before trying again.
settings.esp_flash.readiness.summary.can: The last flash was cancelled. Review the current port selection before starting again.
settings.esp_flash.logs_intro: Build, erase, and upload output appears here while the toolchain runs.
settings.esp_flash.logs_failed_body: Use the failed step and recent-attempt details below to recover, then retry once the board is ready.
settings.esp_flash.logs_idle_body: Start a flash to stream tool output here. If no port is detected yet, reconnect the board and refresh the port list first.
settings.esp_flash.logs_running_body: The job has started. Build and upload output will appear here as soon as the toolchain begins emitting logs.
settings.esp_flash.history_empty_body: Your first successful or failed flash will be recorded here with the target port and result.
settings.wizard.no_gearboxes: No pre-defined gearboxes. Enter specs manually below.
